title: Entrance recommendation explains directional compatibility before proximity
purpose: Make a farther exact directional entrance understandable without allowing the UI to replace route-compatibility hard filters with nearest-IC ranking.

limitations: Facilities, carriageways, ETAs, distances, and route joins are synthetic.
This scenario does not call live location or a surface-routing provider.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.status
then.rationale: One exact directional entrance remains after the hard filters.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.selected_facility_id
then.rationale: The UI presents the route-compatible directional facility rather than the nearest named entrance.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.selection.target_carriageway_id
then.rationale: Directional compatibility names an exact target carriageway.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.join_occurrence_id
then.rationale: The recommendation explains where the approach joins the selected route.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.selection.straight_line_distance_rank
then.rationale: The selected entrance is visibly not the nearest candidate.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.selection.reason_codes
then.rationale: The adapter receives a routing-owned direction reason instead of inferring it from a label.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.selection.reason_codes
then.rationale: Route compatibility remains a hard-filter explanation.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.selection.proximity_only
then.rationale: The UI cannot describe the recommendation as a nearest-entrance choice.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.rejections.test.entry.nearest.westbound
then.rationale: A closer but route-incompatible entrance remains visible with its rejection reason.

then.subject: entry_recommendation.rejections.test.entry.unknown.eastbound
then.rationale: Unknown timed availability fails closed and is not shown as usable.
